title: (Bearish Signal) LPTUSD, Aroon Indicator entered a Downtrend on October 27, 2020.
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
For the last three days, Tickeron A.I.dvisor has detected that LPT.X's AroonDown red line is above 70 while the AroonUp green line is below 30 for three straight days. This move could indicate a strong downtrend ahead for LPT.X, and traders may view it as a Sell signal for the next month. Traders may consider selling the stock or exploring put options. Tickeron A.I.dvisor backtested this indicator and found 102 similar cases, 92 of which were successful. Based on this data, the odds of success are 90%. Current price $1.83 crossed the resistance line at $1.92 and is trading between $1.92 resistance and $1.82 support lines. Throughout the month of 10/05/20 - 10/27/20, the price experienced a -30% Downtrend. During the week of 10/22/20 - 10/27/20, the crypto fell -3%.

https://preview.redd.it/x65lmnorcvv51.png?width=1529&format=png&auto=webp&s=9d63a4803b6fd49845676dc4dc4e644e897946f0

**Bearish Trend Analysis**

The Momentum Indicator moved below the 0 level on September 30, 2020. You may want to consider selling the stock, shorting the stock, or exploring put options on LPT.X as a result. Tickeron A.I. detected that in 26 of 30 cases where the Momentum Indicator fell below 0, the stock fell further within the subsequent month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 87%.

LPT.X moved below its 50-day Moving Average on September 26, 2020 date and that indicates a change from an upward trend to a downward trend.

The 10-day Moving Average for LPT.X crossed bearishly below the 50-day moving average on September 25, 2020. This indicates that the trend has shifted lower and could be considered a sell signal. Tickeron A.I. detected that in 5 of 6 past instances when the 10-day crossed below the 50-day, the stock continued to move higher over the following month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 83%.

Following a 3-day decline, the stock is projected to fall further. Considering past instances where Apple declined for three days, the price rose further in 50 of 62 cases within the following month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 83%.
